title: One-command synthetic harness proof
mission: Provide one offline command that observes the registered AgentSwitchboard harness composition, validates safe nodes and event edges, runs required offline validators, and emits an honest PASS, SKIP, or FAIL matrix without runtime or target mutation.

owner.surface: app harness validation and event topology

safeParallelWork: Unrelated provider runtime work outside app-harness paths
Child-repository plan adoption in separate branches and pull requests
ownedScope: offline aggregate harness validator and root command
registered app-composition node and edge graph
capability, trigger, manifest, agent-contract, and public-plan wiring
forbiddenScope: application, game, browser, provider, agent, installer, or launcher execution
merge, deployment, release, force-push, or default-branch mutation

tasks.title: Register composition nodes and event edges
acceptanceCriteria: Every registered required node has a required edge
Observed and output nodes are reachable from the observer
evidence: Tracked graph and closed schema on feat/one-command-harness-proof

tasks.title: Implement offline aggregate observer
acceptanceCriteria: Detect repository root and print branch and commit
Emit JSON and English matrix outside the repository
Skip optional MCP or LSP readiness honestly
evidence: Tracked root command, observer, result schema, and report renderer

tasks.title: Prove parser, matrix, artifact, and safety contracts
acceptanceCriteria: Matrix and JSON artifacts are emitted
No application, launcher, browser, provider, or network execution is present
evidence: Pending exact-head Windows and Linux CI

validation: git diff --check for the full PR range

proof.target: offline synthetic harness aggregation, registered composition topology, JSON and English artifact emission, and no-mutation exact-head CI
proof.achieved: Tracked implementation is present; exact-head CI is pending.
proof.ceiling: Does not prove live event delivery, application or launcher behavior, provider availability, MCP or LSP service readiness, network behavior, target mutation, deployment, or unregistered nodes and edges.

notes: Open a stacked pull request against feat/public-plans-startup-readiness.
Record exact-head Windows and Linux evidence before marking TEST-01 completed.
Expand the graph only through tracked review when new application nodes or event edges become canonical.
